title: Unify policy and memory decisions in single inline escalation surface
context: Constitution policy decisions (candidate review, activation) and Memory decisions (topic conflicts) required separate handling. Users faced fragmented decision points; no unified surface to see all human call-outs. Touches invariant(s): con_e04226bd05 (The MCP server must stay client-agnostic: all assistants point to the same .hunch/ graph; no Claude-only behavior in the server).
decision: Policy escalations now route through policyEscalations() function (§59.5.3), integrating with pendingEscalations() into one inline escalation mechanism. PolicyLite interface (minimal shape) keeps core dependency-free from Constitution schemas. All human moments surface as QUESTIONS, never approvals.
consequences: Single unified escalation surface reduces cognitive load; users see all decision points (memory + policy) in one place
Policy decisions framed as questions (prove/decide, activate/reject) with explicit human actor recorded on vouch
Fail-open semantics: broken policy store doesn't break memory escalations (try/catch at session-start)
Constitution moments appear inline in session-start orientation and VS Code Hunch view, not as separate queue
Requires PolicyLite abstraction layer to maintain core's architectural independence
alternatives_rejected: Keep policy decisions in separate panel/queue with different UX
Move Constitution service into core (violates layering; creates circular dep)
Machine-approve policies, surface them to human for review after-the-fact
Surface only when explicitly requested (loses inline decision context)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Keep policy decisions in separate panel/queue with different UX
Move Constitution service into core (violates layering; creates circular dep)
Machine-approve policies, surface them to human for review after-the-fact
